summary: A hot GDP print sparked a hawkish open flush, then dip buyers took most of it back. The third estimate of Q1 GDP came in at 2.1% (versus 1.6% consensus) and jobless claims printed a tight 215K, a hawkish combination into tomorrow's PCE, so rate-sensitive MNQ flushed roughly 900 pts at the 9:30 ET open (30,215 to 29,296), broke its long stop intraday, then reclaimed the 1hr trend stop LONG at 29,258 and closed 29,760 (-1.12%) off the lows. MES (-0.58%) never confirmed - it held below its 7,454 stop all session, so the NQ-long / ES-short split is the caution flag. Commodities snapped back: crude +2.26% and gold +0.64% both flipped their 1hr stops long as the Iran risk-premium unwind paused. A two-sided whipsaw into Friday's PCE: trade the level, not the story.

keyLevels.label: MES ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: ES below it; reclaim confirms NQ

setups.name: TSMOM-20 1hr (LONG)
setups.entry: The 1hr ATR trend stop reclaimed long at 29,258; the system is long while hourly closes hold above it
setups.bias: Long re-armed but only after a whipsaw, and into a PCE event open. Size down hard; this is a chop signature, not a clean trend. No chase of the reclaim.

setups.name: AVWAP Deviation Band (MNQ)
setups.entry: A clean -2s 29,610 or +2s 29,918 tag with a volume-exhaustion bar and a center (29,764) reclaim
setups.bias: Today's -3s flush reverted but came in the first event candle (no-touch). Reversion re-qualifies tomorrow only away from the 8:30 print, half size, never first touch.

event.name: Q1 GDP (third estimate), initial jobless claims, May durable goods - 8:30 AM ET
event.consensus: Final Q1 GDP consensus 1.6%; initial jobless claims consensus 225K; advance May durable goods consensus +0.2%.
event.actual: Q1 GDP third estimate +2.1% (revised up 0.5 pt, mainly on lower imports, with consumer spending revised down). Initial jobless claims 215K (below the 225K consensus, a still-tight labor read). Durable goods near consensus.
event.surprise: Growth beat and a tight labor print read hawkish (fewer Fed cuts) into tomorrow's PCE, even though the GDP internals (softer consumer) were less rosy than the headline.
event.nqReaction: MNQ flushed roughly 900 pts at the 9:30 ET cash open (30,215 to 29,296), breaking its long stop intraday (flipped short on the 13:00 UTC bar), then fully reversed: it reclaimed the 1hr trend stop LONG at 29,258 on the 17:00 UTC bar and closed 29,760 (-1.12%). A two-sided whipsaw for TSMOM-20.
event.crossAsset: MES -0.58% to 7,434, still below its 7,454 stop (no long confirmation). MGC +0.64% to 4,042, flipping its 1hr stop long and closing stretched beyond +3s. MCL +2.26% to 71.48, bouncing off 68.87 and flipping long. Equities net red and choppy; commodities re-rated higher as the Iran-premium unwind paused.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap fade)
observe.note: The open gap-down/flush fully reversed. Gap-fade is suspect on an 8:30-data open and doubly so into Friday's PCE; no clean setup today.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ (long-only 252)
observe.note: The longer 252-period trend held long through the intraday whipsaw; today's close does not change the long-only 252 leg.

observe.name: ES confirmation
observe.note: MES is 20.5 pts below its 7,454 stop. An hourly close above 7,454 / 7,500 aligns the equity complex long; until then NQ is long alone and the divergence is a caution flag.

risk.throttleState: REDUCED today, stepping to DEFENSIVE for the 8:30 ET PCE print. TSMOM-20 took a long-short-long whipsaw and ES has not confirmed.
risk.ddProximity: Contained but the whipsaw cost a round-trip. The re-armed long sits 502 pts above its 29,258 stop, a wide buffer, but the re-arm followed a stop-out, so realized chop drag is the real risk, not a gap to the stop.
risk.dailyLossBudget: Most of the budget intact, minus today's whipsaw drag. Do not try to win it back into the PCE print.
risk.sizing: Long-only stack is long again (NQ reclaimed). Size down on a chop-born re-arm into an event open; no new risk into the 8:30 ET PCE, let the first hour resolve. Gold and crude flipped long but the long-only equity stack takes no commodity trades.
risk.note: The binding constraint is revenge-trading the whipsaw or chasing the reclaim into PCE. Let the level (29,258 stop, 29,764 AVWAP) define the long; do not front-run the print. Week ahead: Friday Jun 26 May PCE / Personal Income and Outlays at 8:30 ET and final June UMich at 10:00 ET; Jun 30 quarter-end rebalancing follows.

psychology: A whipsaw is the tax a trend-follower pays - the edge is surviving it by rule, not predicting it. Today the open flush stopped the long out and re-armed it 300 pts lower; anyone who panic-shorted the 29,296 low or revenge-bought the reclaim got chopped twice. Notice what the system did right: the 2-close filter on yesterday's map refused to call a failed flip on a single violent bar, and price reclaimed. The lesson is the same one we teach on every event day - one bar is noise, two closes are signal. Let Friday's PCE come to your levels (29,258 and 29,764), and do not let a whipsaw bait you into trading your P&L instead of your plan.

fullReport.title: Market Regime Snapshot (6-Axis)
fullReport.html:
AxisStateConfidence
DirectionTwo-sided whipsaw, leaning recovery. MNQ closed 29,760 (-1.12%) but the 1hr ATR trend stop was lost on the open flush then RECLAIMED long at 29,258 by the 17:00 UTC bar. MES stayed below its 7,454 stop = NQ long / ES short divergence, not confirmed.MODERATE
VolatilityElevated and expanding. The 9:30 ET open hour swung roughly 900 pts (30,215 high to 29,296 low) before recovering; MNQ ATR(14) 209.HIGH
SessionEvent-open reversal. Hawkish 8:30 data, cash-open flush (OR broke DOWN 13:45 UTC), then a full intraday V-recovery, closed mid-range and back inside the OR.HIGH
InventoryShort / flushed then absorbed. Overnight balanced-to-short (ON low 29,296 = the flush low); opened weak, RTH absorbed and ran +464 off the low.MODERATE
EventPOST_EVENT (Q1 GDP, claims, durables) and SCHEDULED_EVENT tomorrow. Friday Jun 26 is the May PCE / Personal Income and Outlays print at 8:30 ET (the Fed's preferred inflation gauge).HIGH
LiquidityThinning into PCE and quarter-end. Pre-PCE Friday with Jun 30 quarter-end rebalancing flows approaching; expect mechanical, less-clean tape.MODERATE

Cross-asset: equities net red but recovered off the lows (MNQ -1.12%, MES -0.58%), while commodities snapped back (MCL +2.26% and MGC +0.64%, both flipping their 1hr stops long). A hot Q1 GDP (2.1% vs 1.6% consensus) plus tight 215K jobless claims read hawkish into tomorrow's PCE, so rate-sensitive tech flushed at the open, then dip buyers reclaimed the trend stop. It is a near-mirror of Jun 24: equity-led move, commodities on the other side.

fullReport.title: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ)
fullReport.html: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ). OR (13:30 to 13:44 UTC) 29,745.75 to 30,198.5, a very wide 452.75-pt event-open range. Break DOWN at 13:45 UTC: the first 1-min close after the lock printed 29,637, below the OR low, and price extended to 29,296. NYVWAP (session typical-price mean) 29,703; HOF VWAP (anchored at the down-break) 29,691. By the close price had recovered back INSIDE the OR (29,760, above NYVWAP) = a back-into-range reversal. The down-break did not extend; it faded. Lesson: an 8:30-data open makes the first OR break suspect, and today the textbook fade of that first move paid.

fullReport.title: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter)
fullReport.html: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter). The open flush threw the regime filter against longs early (price below the trend EMA, ATR spiking). The afternoon recovery put price back above the EMA and reclaimed the 1hr stop long, but the RVOL surge was front-loaded into the 9:30 flush, not the recovery leg, so the breakout-long lacked clean volume confirmation. Read it as a marginal, chop-born signal rather than a conviction breakout: no chase of the reclaim into tomorrow's PCE, let a pullback to 29,500 / AVWAP set the next entry.

fullReport.title: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ)
fullReport.html: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ). Center (AVWAP) 29,764, sigma 76.9. Bands: +1s 29,841, +2s 29,918, +3s 29,995; -1s 29,687, -2s 29,610, -3s 29,533. The 9:30 flush to 29,296 tagged well BEYOND -3s (29,533) and fully reverted - a textbook exhaustion-and-revert - but it happened in the highest-risk first event candle, the one place the curriculum says do NOT take first touch. Price closed back inside +/-1s (29,760, near the center), so there is no actionable reversion setup into the close. Reversion re-qualifies tomorrow only on a clean -2s/+2s tag with a volume-exhaustion bar and a center reclaim, half size.

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked (Jun 24 map, checked on the Jun 25 session)
fullReport.html:
#Map rowResultMeasured
1MNQ holds above the 29,549 flipped ATR stop on hourly closes (uptrend re-arm, TSMOM stays long)TRIGGERED, MIXEDThe hold broke for one bar on the open flush (13:00 UTC close 29,317, below 29,549), then price reclaimed and closed 29,760 above it; TSMOM re-armed long by the close but took a whipsaw, not a clean hold.
2MNQ stalls at the +2s to +3s band (30,006 to 30,190) into the 8:30 data (stretched-long, reversion to AVWAP 29,678)TRIGGERED, PLAYED OUTMNQ opened in the 30,131 to 30,202 zone, then mean-reverted hard through AVWAP all the way to 29,296, overshooting the 29,678 reversion target. The stretched-long warning nailed it.
3MNQ loses 29,549 and the 29,500 psych on a 2-close basis (flip fails toward -2s 29,268)NOT TRIGGEREDOnly one hourly close printed below 29,500 (the flush bar); the 2-close confirmation never came and price reclaimed. The 2-close filter correctly avoided calling a failed flip on a bear trap.
4MES reclaims and holds its 7,479 stop / 7,500 psych on an hourly close (ES confirms NQ's flip)NOT TRIGGEREDMES never reclaimed its stop; it closed 7,434, below 7,454, and stayed the laggard all session. ES did not confirm.
5MGC rallies into its 4,060 short stop or MCL into its 71.31 short stop and rejects (commodity downtrends intact)TRIGGERED, FAILEDBoth reached their stops (MGC tagged 4,060, MCL ran to 72.5) but neither rejected; both broke and flipped their 1hr stops LONG. The commodity-downtrend-continues thesis failed.

fullReport.title: Tomorrow's Map (for Friday, June 26, 2026)
fullReport.html:
Map for Friday, June 26, 2026. Filters: Friday is not Mon/Wed and PCE is not CPI/FOMC, so BIR, HOF, MID and BNC are all eligible (the HOF 12:00 to 14:00 ET dead zone still applies). Calendar: the marquee print is May PCE / Personal Income and Outlays at 8:30 AM ET (the Fed's preferred inflation gauge), with final June UMich sentiment at 10:00 AM ET; Jun 30 quarter-end rebalancing sits just ahead. Treat the 8:30 ET PCE like an event open: the first OR break is suspect (today's down-break faded). All levels from today's 1hr chart.

IFTHENBIAS
MNQ holds the re-armed 29,258 ATR stop and reclaims AVWAP 29,764 on hourly closes after the 8:30 PCEThe whipsaw long stays armed, TSMOM-20 holds; 30,000 psych is the upside magnetFailed retests that hold 29,258 keep the long on side; a clean reclaim of 29,764 then 30,000 is the green light. Only a 2-close break back below 29,258 voids the re-armed long
MNQ rejects AVWAP 29,764 and stalls below the +2s 29,918 into PCERange-bound under VWAP, mean-reversion risk back toward NYVWAP 29,703 then the 29,500 psychFading the first PCE spike is the trap; dev-band reversion is eligible only on a failed high plus an AVWAP reclaim, half size, never first touch on the print
MNQ loses 29,500 and the 29,258 stop on a 2-close basis after PCEThe re-arm fails, back toward today's 29,296 flush low and the -2s 29,610 shelfA failed re-arm is the tell; treat 29,258 reclaim attempts as supply if it breaks on two closes
MES reclaims and holds its 7,454 stop / 7,500 psych on an hourly closeES finally confirms NQ, the equity complex aligns longAn hourly close above 7,500 is the both-aligned green light; below 7,454 keeps ES the laggard and the NQ/ES split a caution flag
MGC holds its flipped 3,992 stop and MCL holds its flipped 70.63 stop on hourly closesThe commodity re-rate higher continues, the risk-premium unwind stays pausedFailed retests that hold those stops are continuation; only hourly closes back below void the fresh commodity longs

yesterdaysMapScore.note: The GDP-beat whipsaw day. Row 2 (stretched-long reversion to AVWAP) TRIGGERED and PLAYED OUT - the open mean-reverted hard through AVWAP to 29,296. Row 5 (commodity shorts reject) TRIGGERED and FAILED - gold and crude broke their stops and flipped long. Row 1 (hold above 29,549) TRIGGERED and MIXED - lost for one bar on the flush then reclaimed. Row 3 (2-close break of 29,500) NOT TRIGGERED - only one close below, the filter avoided the bear trap. Row 4 (ES reclaims) NOT TRIGGERED - ES stayed below its stop.
